name: Cantabrian Route Natural Trail
name_original: Acantilados de Papel y Punta Arxente desde Porto de Morás
description: Jog the 4.1-mile Paper Cliffs and Punta Arxente coastal path from Porto de Morás, featuring unique geological formations and Atlantic views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Xove traverse a diverse landscape where the mountains meet the sea, offering varied terrain for runners. The region features a rugged coastline with beaches and dramatic cliffs, alongside inland areas with hills and rivers. This combination provides a range of running experiences, from coastal paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation changes. Best jogging routes around Xove The most popular jogging route is Penedo do Galo - circular from Viveiro, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 29 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain towards a prominent viewpoint. Another top favourite among local runners is Cantabrian Route Natural Trail, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This route follows a natural trail, offering coastal scenery and accessible running. Local runners also love the View of Illa de Sarón – View of Isla Ansarón loop from Xuances, a 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail leading through coastal and inland landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Jogging around Xove is defined by its beautiful coastlines, rugged cliffs, and accessible hills. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easier coastal runs to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Xove are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 4 reviews. Nearly 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Xove's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Xove?
A: Xove offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 80 routes available. These range from easy coastal paths to more challenging mountain trails, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes for beginners in Xove?
A: Yes, Xove has several options suitable for beginners. There are 3 easy routes, and 37 moderate routes that can also be a good starting point. For a moderate coastal experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991005582/cantabrian-route-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantabrian Route Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 km long and offers scenic views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Xove's running trails?
A: Xove's running trails feature a diverse landscape where mountains meet the sea. You'll find routes along stunning coastlines with white sand beaches and dramatic cliffs, as well as inland paths through hills and river valleys. This variety provides options from flat, refreshing beach runs to challenging trail runs with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer great viewpoints in Xove?
A: Absolutely! Xove is known for its breathtaking views. Many routes incorporate coastal paths and elevated sections. For spectacular panoramic views of the Bay of Biscay, consider trails near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3173953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tixoso Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990431084/penedo-do-galo-circular-from-viveiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedo do Galo - circular from Viveiro&lt;/a&gt; route also leads to a prominent viewpoint.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Xove?
A: Yes, many of Xove's running paths are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Illa de Sarón – View of Isla Ansarón loop from Xuances&lt;/a&gt;, which is a longer, challenging loop through coastal and inland landscapes.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to see while running in Xove?
A: While running in Xove, you can encounter several natural wonders. The rugged coastline features dramatic formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4617323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paper Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point&lt;/a&gt;. You might also discover hidden gems like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maiden's Cave&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6453869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Souto da Retorta (Retorta Grove)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks near the running trails in Xove?
A: Yes, Xove and its surroundings offer some interesting historical landmarks. You might come across the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3173944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gate of Charles V&lt;/a&gt;, a significant historical site. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3171884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Atalaia Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; also stands as a notable man-made monument offering great views.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Xove?
A: The running trails in Xove are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.0 stars from 4 reviews. Nearly 1000 runners have explored the area, often praising the diverse terrain, from beautiful coastlines to challenging hills, and the fresh air experience.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced trail runners in Xove?
A: For experienced trail runners, Xove offers plenty of challenging options. There are 43 difficult routes, many of which involve significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31436677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rooster's Rock – Gate of Charles V loop from Viveiro-Apeadoiro&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 22.4 km with substantial ascent, provide a rigorous workout.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Xove?
A: One of the longest and most challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Illa de Sarón – View of Isla Ansarón loop from Xuances&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult trail covers approximately 30.4 km (18.9 miles) and typically takes around 3 hours and 44 minutes to complete, leading through varied coastal and inland landscapes.

Q: Are there running routes that pass by beaches in Xove?
A: Yes, Xove's coastline is dotted with beautiful beaches, and many running routes offer the chance to jog alongside them. You can find routes near beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3727364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Román Beach&lt;/a&gt;, Portocelo Beach, Lago Beach, and Morás Beach, providing refreshing coastal runs with fine sand and clear waters.

Q: What kind of elevation changes can I expect on Xove's running trails?
A: The elevation changes on Xove's running trails vary significantly. While coastal routes might be relatively flat, many trails, especially those venturing inland or towards viewpoints, feature considerable ascents and descents. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Penedo do Galo loop from Viveiro-Apeadoiro&lt;/a&gt; includes over 500 meters of elevation gain, offering a good challenge.
tips: Beautiful cliffs located near the point of Morás. With a height of about 40 meters, these granite rock formations, sculpted by the wind and the waves of the sea, owe their name to their appearance in the form of folds reminiscent of sheets of paper.

On the Xove coast, following the path, is Cabo de Morás, a natural wonder sculpted by the force of the Cantabrian Sea hitting the coast.
The loud sound of the water hitting the rocks, together with the spectacular views of the Farallóns islets to the east and As Salseiras and the Ansarón islet to the west, invite you to admire its beauty.
A perfect point from which to contemplate the island of Ansarón, one of the largest on the Lugo coast with some 10 hectares of extension.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Paper Cliffs and Punta Arxente trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically find a gravel parking area near Porto de Morás, which serves as the starting point for the coastal path. It's advisable to arrive early during peak times to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the Paper Cliffs and Punta Arxente trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it's part of a highly protected natural space (ZEC, ZEPA, LIC), it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife, especially seabirds, and to respect other trail users. Please also remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this coastal route is during spring or early autumn. The weather is usually milder, and the views are stunning. Summer can be pleasant, but it might be busier, and winter can bring strong winds and rain, making the uneven terrain more challenging.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Paper Cliffs and Punta Arxente trail. However, please be aware that the area is designated as a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA Costa da Mariña Occidental) and a Site of Community Importance (LIC), so it's essential to stay on marked paths and respect the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Paper Cliffs and Punta Arxente trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by natural, uneven granite rock formations, which can include outcrops, veins, hollows, and cracks. It's a coastal path, not paved, so expect varied ground underfoot. Trail running shoes with good grip are highly recommended for stability and comfort.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the route is considered an 'easy walk' for hikers, for jogging, it presents a moderate challenge due to its uneven natural granite surfaces. Beginners accustomed to road running might find it more demanding, requiring careful foot placement. If you're new to trail jogging, take it slow and enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route around the Paper Cliffs and Punta Arxente from Porto de Morás is typically done as a circular trail, allowing you to experience the diverse coastal scenery without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter breathtaking panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean and the rugged Galician coastline. Key highlights include the unique Paper Cliffs with their distinctive geological formations, the dramatic Cabo de Morás, and stunning View of Isla Ansarón. You might also spot the Farallóns islets, the rocky ridge of As Salseiras, and historical remnants like an old whaling factory ramp and a 'cemetery of breakwaters'.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the Camiño natural do Roteiro do Cantábrico and the Mtb Camiño natural do Roteiro do Cantábrico. You'll also find it intersecting with parts of the Camiño da Salseira, Camiño de Fontamar, and Camiño da Pedrola.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the uneven granite terrain, sturdy trail running shoes with good grip are essential. Dress in layers suitable for coastal weather, which can change quickly. Bring water, especially on warmer days, and consider a light backpack for essentials. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also advisable due to exposure along the coast.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near Porto de Morás?
faqs.answer: Porto de Morás is a small area, but you can find local establishments in nearby towns like Xove or Foz. It's a good idea to check in advance for opening hours, especially outside of peak tourist season.
